'''''Darth Maul'' 1''' is the first issue in the [[Star Wars: Darth Maul (2000)|''Star Wars: Darth Maul'']] series of comics. It was published on [[September 6]], [[2000]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

==Publisher's summary==
''In hiding for generations, the Sith have waited for the precise moment to reveal themselves to the galaxy. Hoping to catch the Jedi unaware, Lord Sidious and his deadly apprentice, Darth Maul, have planned and waited patiently for their moment to pounce. Unfortunately, in the days before the Trade Federation blockade of the planet Naboo, Lord Sidious becomes aware of a threat to his plans. This threat comes from the deadly galactic crime organization Black Sun. But Sidious won't stand for any opposition, and Darth Maul is sent to deal with Black Sun—as only a master of the dark side can!''

==Plot summary==
In the [[LiMerge Building/Legends|LiMerge Building]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Darth Maul undergoes a trial by cutting through and destroying various [[Droid/Legends|droids]] who are attacking with lethal force. Maul is successful in completing the trial, and directly after [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] informs him of a task. With plans for the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion of Naboo]] in place, Sidious worries of his dependence on the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidians]] and the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. Despite this, Sidious wants no interference from any outside forces, and orders his apprentice to strike at the heart of the criminal organization [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]]. To aid Maul in his task, Sidious gives him an armed [[Scimitar/Legends|Star Courier]] that has been redubbed a "Sith Infiltrator" for his apprentice's use. The starship includes a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking generator]] for invisibility as well as a [[Bloodfin/Legends|speeder bike]] and a trio of [[DRK-1 Dark Eye probe droid/Legends|Dark Eye probe droids]]. As Maul leaves Coruscant, [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] senses his dark presence through a disturbance in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] but is unsure of what he has sensed. Jinn tells his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] that even though [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] beings do not always understand what is being told to them, he is certain the meaning will eventually reveal itself.

==Sources==
*[[Star Wars Insider 51|''Star Wars Insider'' 51]] {{Advert}}
*The timeline for this series was originally cited as 2:10 from a reference associated with the [[Great ReSynchronization calendar]],<ref name="Date">{{DarthMaulComics2000Date}}</ref>, which had originated from ''[[West End Games]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' back in 1994. This had listed the [[Clone Wars/Legends|The Clone Wars]] as having ''ended '' in [[35 BBY/Legends|35 BBY]]. It is now commonly acknowledged the Clone Wars would occur a decade later, between [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. This reference is arguably now problematic as a valid source, however, it has been moved to this section for posterity.
